justifications.clarity: The two-channel framework (training vs. inference) is introduced crisply and consistently applied; definitions are precise (Definition 15.1 memorisation, Definition 15.2 DP) and transitions between subsections are smooth throughout.
justifications.rigor: Key results are formally stated with citations (Gaussian mechanism cites Dwork & Roth 2014; advanced composition formula includes the full k*eps*(e^eps-1) term); the proof is labelled a sketch; minor gap: FedAvg section does not state convergence assumptions under data heterogeneity (non-IID clients).
justifications.completeness: All seven approved outline sections are covered, all eight learning objectives are addressed in corresponding sections, and the compliance table in Section 7.2 maps each regulatory obligation to a specific mitigation and evidence type.
justifications.pedagogy: Context boxes motivate each section narratively before the technical content; difficulty progression is appropriate (conceptual framing → threat identification → practical mitigations → advanced techniques → system design → evaluation); three financial-domain examples (routing architecture, NER pipeline, privacy-aware RAG) are concrete and specific.
justifications.style: No paragraph-level heading labels; citet/citep usage is consistent with the authoryear biblatex style; theorem/definition/remark/context/deepdive environments are applied appropriately; compliance table uses booktabs correctly.
